name: Degenerative Joint Disease (Osteoarthritis)
description: Osteoarthritis is progressive deterioration of joint cartilage causing pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility. It is the end result of many orthopaedic conditions and is extremely common in senior dogs. It significantly impacts quality of life if poorly managed.
treatment_overview: Multimodal management including NSAIDs, joint supplements (fish oil, green-lipped mussel), physiotherapy, hydrotherapy, weight management, and environmental adaptations (ramps, orthopaedic beds). Intra-articular therapies and monoclonal antibodies (bedinvetmab) are emerging options.
prevention_tips: Joint supplements from middle age in large breeds
